upper_intermediate.title: Clauses of Contrast and Purpose
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 Clauses of CONTRAST – Mệnh đề đối lập
1. although / even though + mệnh đề (S + V)
Dùng ở đầu hoặc giữa câu. KHÔNG dùng dấu phẩy sau although/even though.
Although it was raining, we went for a walk.
We went for a walk, although it was raining.
Even though she was tired, she kept working. (nhấn mạnh hơn although)

2. however – nối hai câu riêng biệt
Dùng sau dấu chấm (.) hoặc chấm phẩy (;). LUÔN có dấu phẩy sau however.
We didn't like the hotel. However, we had a fantastic time.
We went to the beach; however, the weather wasn't perfect.

3. despite / in spite of + danh từ hoặc V-ing
Dùng ở đầu hoặc giữa câu. KHÔNG dùng trực tiếp với mệnh đề S+V.
Despite the rain, we went to the concert.
In spite of feeling tired, she finished her work.
We went out despite the cold weather.

4. despite / in spite of + the fact that + mệnh đề
Khi muốn dùng với mệnh đề đầy đủ:
We went out despite the fact that it was raining.

5. while / whereas – đối lập hai sự việc / quan điểm
Dùng ở đầu hoặc giữa câu, sau dấu phẩy.
While/Whereas she enjoys cooking, he prefers to eat out.
Mary likes classical music, while/whereas her sister prefers jazz.
📌 Clauses of PURPOSE – Mệnh đề chỉ mục đích
1. to + infinitive (phổ biến nhất)
She studies hard to pass the exam.
He left early to avoid traffic.

2. in order to / so as to + infinitive (trang trọng hơn)
We were asked to stay in order to finish the project.
Use a plastic hammer so as to avoid damage.

3. in order not to / so as not to + infinitive (phủ định)
He left home early in order not to be late.
She spoke quietly so as not to wake the baby.

4. so that + S + modal (can/could/would/won't…)
Dùng khi chủ ngữ của hai vế khác nhau, hoặc muốn nhấn mạnh kết quả mong muốn.
He whispered so that nobody could hear him.
She saved money so that she could travel abroad.
I wrote it down so that I wouldn't forget.
⚠️ Những lỗi thường gặp
❌ Sai✅ Đúng
Despite he was tired, he continued.Despite being tired, he continued.
He whispered so that to avoid attention.He whispered so that nobody noticed. / to avoid attention.
Even though she tried but she failed.Even though she tried, she failed.
Although, she was tired.Although she was tired, ... (không có dấy phẩy sau although)
She took an umbrella in case it rained, although the sky looked clear.Mục đích (in case) + đối lập (although) trong cùng một câu phức
Despite working long hours, he never missed his son's school events.despite + V-ing → không cần chủ ngữ
He turned down the music so that his neighbours wouldn't complain.so that + S + modal (wouldn't) → mục đích với chủ ngữ khác

exercises.q: ___ the heavy traffic, we arrived on time.
exercises.exp: Despite + danh từ (the heavy traffic) → mệnh đề đối lập

exercises.q: She kept smiling ___ she was feeling terrible inside.
exercises.exp: even though + S+V → đối lập, nhấn mạnh sự tương phản mạnh

exercises.q: He left work early ___ avoid the rush-hour traffic.
exercises.exp: in order to + infinitive → mệnh đề chỉ mục đích (trang trọng)

exercises.q: The film was very long. ___, I enjoyed every minute of it.
exercises.exp: However nối hai câu riêng biệt, luôn theo sau là dấy phẩy

exercises.q: She turned down the TV ___ her baby could sleep.
exercises.opts: so that,so as to,in order to,despite
exercises.exp: so that + S + modal (could) → mục đích khi chủ ngữ hai vế khác nhau

exercises.q: ___ working very hard, he failed the exam.
exercises.exp: In spite of + V-ing → đối lập (= despite)

exercises.q: She prefers tea, ___ her husband drinks coffee every morning.
exercises.exp: whereas → đối lập hai sự thật khác nhau, dùng giữa câu

exercises.q: He spoke slowly ___ everyone could understand him clearly.
exercises.exp: so that + S + modal → mục đích với chủ ngữ khác (everyone)

exercises.q: She wore a coat ___ keep warm in the cold weather.
exercises.exp: to + infinitive → mục đích đơn giản, cùng chủ ngữ

exercises.q: ___ the bad reviews, the restaurant was always full.
exercises.exp: Despite + danh từ (the bad reviews) → đối lập

exercises.q: I wrote his number down ___ I wouldn't forget it.
exercises.exp: so that + S + wouldn't → mục đích phủ định, chủ ngữ giống nhau nhưng nhấn mạnh kết quả

exercises.q: He used headphones ___ not disturb anyone in the library.
exercises.exp: so as not to → mục đích phủ định, trang trọng

exercises.q: ___ she had studied a lot, she still felt nervous before the test.
exercises.exp: Although + S+V → đối lập ở đầu câu, không có dấu phẩy sau although

exercises.q: I enjoy swimming. My sister, ___, hates the water.
exercises.exp: however nối hai câu, thường ở giữa câu thứ hai, theo sau là dấu phẩy

exercises.q: She left the party early ___ feeling ill.
exercises.exp: despite + V-ing → chứ không phải despite + that

exercises.q: He trained every day ___ he could win the championship.
exercises.exp: so that + modal (could) → mục đích

exercises.q: Tom is very outgoing, ___ his twin brother is extremely shy.
exercises.exp: whereas → đối chiếu hai tính cách/đặc điểm trái ngược

exercises.q: She left a note ___ her family would know where she was.
exercises.exp: so that + S + modal (would) → mục đích với chủ ngữ khác

exercises.q: ___ the fact that it was freezing, they went jogging.
exercises.exp: despite the fact that + S+V → dùng khi despite đứng trước mệnh đề đầy đủ

exercises.q: She whispered ___ not wake the sleeping children.
exercises.exp: so as to + infinitive → mục đích trang trọng, cùng chủ ngữ

exercises.q: ___ (mặc dù) it was very expensive, she bought the dress.
exercises.exp: Although/Even though + S+V → mệnh đề đối lập ở đầu câu

exercises.q: He whispered ___ (để không làm) wake the sleeping baby.
exercises.ans: so as not to / in order not to
exercises.exp: so as not to / in order not to + infinitive → diễn đạt mục đích tránh việc gì đó

exercises.q: She took an umbrella ___ (mặc dù) the forecast said it would be sunny.
exercises.exp: even though / although + mệnh đề → diễn đạt sự tương phản dù dự báo thời tiết đẹp

exercises.q: ___ (Bất chấp) the heavy traffic, she arrived on time.
exercises.exp: Despite/In spite of + danh từ/V-ing → đối lập, không dùng mệnh đề đầy đủ sau

exercises.q: He left the party early ___ (để) he could catch the last train home.
exercises.exp: so that + S + could/would/can → diễn đạt mục đích

exercises.q: She is very outgoing, ___ (trong khi đó) her brother is extremely shy.
exercises.exp: whereas / while → so sánh tương phản hai vế đối lập nhau trong cùng câu

exercises.q: He studied medicine ___ (để có thể) help people in his village.
exercises.ans: so that he could / in order to
exercises.exp: so that + modal hoặc in order to + infinitive → mệnh đề mục đích

exercises.q: ___ (mặc dù) all the difficulties, she never gave up.
exercises.exp: Despite/In spite of + danh từ → đối lập

exercises.q: She goes to bed early ___ (để) she can wake up fresh.
exercises.exp: so that + S + modal (can) → mục đích

exercises.q: He likes hiking, ___ (trong khi đó) his partner prefers staying indoors.
exercises.exp: whereas/while → đối lập hai sự thật, ở giữa câu

exercises.q: The meeting went well. ___ (Tuy nhiên), there are still some issues to resolve.
exercises.exp: However → nối hai câu, theo sau là dấu phẩy

exercises.q: She wore sunglasses ___ (để tránh) being recognised by fans.
exercises.ans: so as to avoid / in order to avoid / to avoid
exercises.exp: to/so as to/in order to + infinitive → mục đích

exercises.q: ___ (mặc dù) feeling exhausted, he continued to work.
exercises.exp: Despite/In spite of + V-ing → không dùng S+V trực tiếp sau despite

exercises.q: She saved money ___ (để) she could travel around Europe.
exercises.exp: so that + could → mục đích trong quá khứ

exercises.q: He took notes ___ (để không bỏ lỡ) any important details.
exercises.ans: so as not to miss / in order not to miss
exercises.exp: so as not to / in order not to → mục đích phủ định

exercises.q: The presentation was clear ___ (mặc dù) the topic was very complex.
exercises.exp: although/even though + S+V → đối lập ở cuối câu

exercises.q: He drove slowly ___ (để) the children would be safe.
exercises.exp: so that + S + would → mục đích với chủ ngữ khác

exercises.q: She loves the city, ___ (trong khi đó) I prefer the countryside.
exercises.exp: whereas/while → đối lập hai quan điểm trái nhau

exercises.q: He lowered his voice ___ (để không làm) disturb his colleagues.
exercises.ans: so as not to / in order not to
exercises.exp: in order not to / so as not to + infinitive → mục đích phủ định

exercises.q: The team won the match ___ (mặc dù sự thật là) the star player was injured.
exercises.exp: despite the fact that + S+V → dùng khi muốn dùng mệnh đề đầy đủ sau despite

reorder.words: although it was raining we decided to go for a walk
she kept working despite feeling very tired and unwell
he left early in order to avoid the heavy traffic
the hotel was expensive however we had a wonderful stay
she turned down the music so that her baby could sleep
in spite of the bad weather the festival was a great success
he wore gloves so as not to leave fingerprints on the surface
whereas she enjoys city life he prefers living in the countryside
despite the fact that it was late she continued to work on the report
she studied every night so that she could pass the entrance exam
even though he had little experience he performed extremely well
she spoke slowly and clearly so that everyone could understand
he uses a notepad in order not to forget important information
the company expanded rapidly while its competitors struggled to survive
however the project was challenging the team delivered it on time
she took a taxi so as to arrive at the meeting on time
despite working overtime every day she never completed the project
he wore a hat and scarf in order not to be recognised
whereas Tom is very sociable his twin brother is quite reserved
she left a message so that he would know where to find her

passage.text: Maria had always dreamed of becoming a doctor, {{1}} she knew the training would take many years. She worked part-time {{2}} she could pay her university fees. {{3}} the long hours of study, she never lost her motivation. Her friends often went out at weekends; {{4}}, Maria usually stayed home to revise. She wore earplugs {{5}} the noise of the city would not distract her. {{6}} her grades were sometimes disappointing, she refused to give up. She made detailed notes after every lecture {{7}} she could review them later. {{8}} the difficult first year, she passed all her exams. Her parents were not wealthy. {{9}}, they supported her as much as they could. By her final year, she was studying twelve hours a day {{10}} finish her thesis on time.

The new city park was built {{1}} residents could enjoy outdoor activities. {{2}} some local people objected to the development at first, the majority now love it. The park is open all year, {{3}} the opening hours change in winter. Some visitors come {{4}} exercise, {{5}} others come simply to relax. {{6}} the cold weather, hundreds of people visit every weekend. The council installed extra lighting {{7}} families could feel safe after dark. There are also quiet reading areas {{8}} people who prefer peace and quiet. {{9}}, those who prefer noise and activity can use the sports courts. {{10}} the park being free, it is kept extremely clean and well-maintained.

James decided to learn Spanish {{1}} he could communicate better during his travels. {{2}} being a complete beginner, he made rapid progress in the first month. He signed up for an online course {{3}} he could study at his own pace. {{4}} his busy work schedule, he found time to practise every evening. He listened to Spanish podcasts {{5}} not fall behind. His colleague Maria speaks fluent Spanish, {{6}} her husband cannot say a single word. James kept a vocabulary notebook {{7}} he could review new words easily. {{8}} he made many mistakes at first, he gradually became more confident. He even watched films without subtitles {{9}} improve his listening. {{10}}, he is now able to hold basic conversations with native speakers.

The scientists worked around the clock {{1}} finish the research before the deadline. {{2}} facing countless setbacks, they never considered abandoning the project. The lead researcher preferred early mornings, {{3}} her assistant worked best late at night. She kept detailed records {{4}} future scientists could build on their work. {{5}} there was very little funding, the team achieved remarkable results. They published their findings in a simple format {{6}} non-scientists could also understand them. {{7}} the complexity of the subject, the article became very popular online. The director praised the team. {{8}}, he admitted that the budget for the next phase would be cut. Extra volunteers were recruited {{9}} the workload would be shared. {{10}} working with limited resources, the team continued to produce groundbreaking results.

The charity was set up {{1}} homeless people could access food and shelter. {{2}} limited resources, the volunteers managed to help over 500 people in the first year. They organised fundraising events {{3}} they could expand their services. {{4}} some donors gave generously, others refused to contribute at all. Volunteers gave up their weekends {{5}} help with the outreach programme. They kept costs low {{6}} more money could go directly to those in need. {{7}} the growing demand, the charity never turned anyone away. The founder preferred to work behind the scenes, {{8}} the deputy director was always in front of the cameras. The charity published monthly reports {{9}} donors could see how their money was spent. {{10}} the enormous challenges, the team remained deeply committed to their mission.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một quyết định khó khăn bạn đã từng đưa ra – mặc dù có nhiều trở ngại nhưng bạn vẫn thực hiện và kết quả ra sao (sử dụng although, despite, however, so that, in order to, whereas)
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Clauses of contrast (although, despite, however, whereas) and purpose (so that, in order to, to)
writing_prompts.example: One of the most difficult decisions I have ever made was to leave a well-paid job in order to pursue my passion for teaching. Although the salary was excellent, I felt deeply unfulfilled every single day. My family was worried, whereas my closest friends were supportive from the very beginning. I saved money for months so that I could afford to retrain without going into debt. Despite having no teaching experience, I enrolled in a postgraduate education programme. It was exhausting, and I had very little free time; however, I never once regretted my choice. I studied late into the night so as not to fall behind the other students. Even though the first year was incredibly demanding, I qualified with distinction, and I have loved every day in the classroom since.

writing_prompts.vi: So sánh cuộc sống ở thành phố và nông thôn, nêu ưu và nhược điểm của mỗi nơi. Dùng whereas/while để đối chiếu, và các liên từ mục đích để giải thích lý do tại sao người ta chọn mỗi nơi.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Whereas/while for contrast; so that, in order to, to for purpose
writing_prompts.example: City life and rural life offer very different experiences, and people choose between them for a wide variety of reasons. Many people move to cities in order to access better job opportunities and higher salaries. City dwellers often enjoy excellent public transport and cultural attractions; however, they frequently complain about noise, pollution, and the high cost of living. Whereas urban residents can reach a hospital or a theatre within minutes, people living in the countryside often have to travel long distances to access even basic services. Despite this, many people deliberately choose rural life so that they can enjoy peace, fresh air, and a stronger sense of community. Some families move to the countryside so as not to raise their children in a crowded urban environment. While city life suits ambitious professionals, the countryside attracts those who value a slower and quieter way of living.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một chuyến đi du lịch mà bạn đã từng thực hiện — mô tả những khó khăn gặp phải, điều bạn thích và điều bạn không thích. Dùng các từ nối đối lập và mục đích (although, whereas, despite, so that, in order to, however).
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Contrast clauses (although, despite, whereas, however) and purpose clauses (so that, in order to)
writing_prompts.example: Last summer, I travelled to Japan with two friends, even though none of us spoke the language. We had studied a few basic phrases in order to communicate with locals, and downloaded a translation app so that we could read menus and signs. Despite the language barrier, we managed to get around surprisingly well. The cities were incredibly busy and crowded, whereas the smaller towns we visited were calm and peaceful. We got lost several times; however, this often led us to discover wonderful hidden restaurants and temples. Although the trip was exhausting at times, it was one of the most rewarding experiences of my life. I would go back without hesitation, in spite of the challenges we faced.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy kể về một thói quen lành mạnh bạn đã cố gắng xây dựng — tại sao bạn bắt đầu, những khó khăn nào bạn gặp phải, và kết quả của việc kiên trì. Dùng các từ nối đối lập và mục đích (although, despite, in order to, so that, however, whereas).
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Contrast and purpose clauses in narrative writing
writing_prompts.example: About two years ago, I decided to start exercising regularly in order to improve my overall health and energy levels. Although I had tried to form this habit before, I had always given up after a few weeks. This time, I set small, achievable goals so that I would not feel overwhelmed from the start. I went to the gym three times a week, whereas before I had barely walked to the end of my street. The early mornings were difficult; however, I reminded myself of my reasons for starting whenever I felt like quitting. Despite being tired after long shifts at work, I never allowed myself to skip more than one session in a row so as not to lose momentum. After three months, the results were remarkable. I slept better, felt stronger, and was far more productive during the day, even though I had expected the process to take much longer.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một người bạn ngưỡng mộ – họ đã vượt qua khó khăn như thế nào và làm những gì để đạt được thành công (dùng đầy đủ các cấu trúc đối lập và mục đích)
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Contrast: although, even though, despite, however; Purpose: so that, in order to, so as to
writing_prompts.example: The person I admire most is my uncle, who built a successful business from nothing despite having dropped out of school at the age of fifteen. Although he had no formal qualifications, he taught himself everything he needed to know about engineering. He worked two jobs simultaneously in order to save enough money to open his first workshop. Even though he faced rejection from several banks, he never gave up. He kept his prices competitive so that customers would choose him over larger rivals. Whereas most entrepreneurs in his field gave up after the first setback, he treated every failure as a learning opportunity. He hired local young people so as not to take his success away from the community that had supported him. However, perhaps the most impressive thing about him is his humility — despite everything he has achieved, he remains one of the most modest people I have ever met.

upper_intermediate.title: Questions: Different Types
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 1. Câu hỏi thông thường (Direct Questions)
Cấu trúc: Trợ động từ / Modal + S + V chính
Nếu không có trợ động từ → dùng do/does (hiện tại đơn) hoặc did (quá khứ đơn).
Where do you live? / What did she say?
Have you finished? / Can you help me?

Giới từ trong câu hỏi:
📌 Văn nói/không trang trọng: giới từ đặt ở cuối câu
Who did you talk to? / What is she interested in?
📌 Văn viết/trang trọng: giới từ đặt trước từ hỏi
To whom did you speak? / In what are you interested?
📌 2. Subject Questions – Câu hỏi với chủ ngữ là từ hỏi
Khi who / what / which là chủ ngữ của câu hỏi → KHÔNG đảo ngược, KHÔNG dùng do/does/did.
Who called you? (= Ai đã gọi cho bạn? → who là chủ ngữ)
What happened? / Which team won?

So sánh:
Who did you call? (= Bạn đã gọi cho ai? → you là S, who là tân ngữ)
Who called you? (= Ai đã gọi bạn? → who là S)
📌 3. Negative Questions – Câu hỏi phủ định
Dùng để xác nhận điều mình cho là đúng, hoặc bày tỏ sự ngạc nhiên/thất vọng.
Didn't you like the film? (= Tôi nghĩ bạn thích bộ phim đó.)
Isn't she coming? (= Tôi nghĩ cô ấy sẽ đến.)
Haven't you finished yet? (= Tôi bắt đầu mất kiên nhẫn.)
📌 4. Tag Questions – Câu hỏi đuôi
Dùng để xác nhận thông tin hoặc mời đồng ý.
✦ Câu khẳng định + đuôi phủ định: She's coming, isn't she?
✦ Câu phủ định + đuôi khẳng định: You don't smoke, do you?
✦ Một số trường hợp đặc biệt:
I am right, aren't I? / Let's go, shall we? / Don't be late, will you?
There's a problem, isn't there? / Nobody called, did they?
⚠️ Những lỗi thường gặp
❌ Sai✅ Đúng
What he said? / What she does?What did he say? / What does she do?
Who did call you? (subject question)Who called you?
She's a teacher, isn't it?She's a teacher, isn't she?
Nobody came, didn't they?Nobody came, did they?
Who broke the window? – Subject question, không dùng 'did'who = chủ ngữ → không đảo ngược trật tự
Who did you speak to? – Object question với giới từ ở cuốiyou = chủ ngữ, who = tân ngữ → dùng did
You've met her before, haven't you? – Tag questioncâu khẳng định (have) → đuôi phủ định (haven't)

exercises.q: ___ called you earlier? – It was my brother.
exercises.exp: Subject question: who = chủ ngữ → không dùng did, không đảo ngược

exercises.q: She left the meeting early, ___ she?
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định (past simple: left) → đuôi phủ định (didn't)

exercises.q: What ___ you ___ at the weekend?
exercises.exp: Hỏi về quá khứ → did + V nguyên mẫu

exercises.q: ___ you speak to at the conference?
exercises.exp: Object question: you = chủ ngữ, who(m) = tân ngữ → dùng did

exercises.q: Nobody told you about the meeting, ___ they?
exercises.exp: Nobody = phủ định → đuôi khẳng định (did they)

exercises.q: ___ won the match last night?
exercises.opts: Which team,Which team did,Which team does,What team did
exercises.exp: Subject question: which team = chủ ngữ → không đảo, không dùng did

exercises.q: ___ she interested in?
exercises.opts: What is,What does,What is she,What she is
exercises.exp: Hỏi về tân ngữ của 'interested in' → What is she interested in?

exercises.q: You haven't eaten yet, ___ you?
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định (haven't) → đuôi khẳng định (have you)

exercises.q: ___ she always arrive late? I'm getting frustrated.
exercises.exp: Negative question → bày tỏ sự thất vọng/ngạc nhiên (Doesn't she always...)

exercises.q: Let's take a break, ___ we?
exercises.exp: Let's... → đuôi đặc biệt: shall we?

exercises.q: What ___ happen during the meeting?
exercises.exp: Subject question: what = chủ ngữ → dùng did (quá khứ) nhưng không đảo; đây là trường hợp 'what happened' nhưng với thời gian cụ thể → did là đúng cho câu bình thường. Chú ý: 'What happened' (không did) cũng đúng với subject question

exercises.q: You'll be there on time, ___ you?
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định (will) → đuôi phủ định (won't you)

exercises.q: ___ caused the accident? – A burst tyre.
exercises.exp: Subject question: what = chủ ngữ → không dùng did/was

exercises.q: Nobody has complained, ___ they?
exercises.exp: Nobody = phủ định → đuôi khẳng định (have they)

exercises.q: ___ you waiting for? I've been here for twenty minutes.
exercises.exp: Hỏi tân ngữ trong past continuous → What were you waiting for?

exercises.q: You'd rather stay home, ___ you?
exercises.exp: You'd rather (= would rather) → đuôi: wouldn't you

exercises.q: ___ gave you permission to leave early?
exercises.exp: Subject question: who = chủ ngữ (gave) → không dùng did

exercises.q: There's been a mistake, ___ there?
exercises.exp: There's been (= there has been) → đuôi: hasn't there

exercises.q: ___ she live before moving to London?
exercises.exp: Hỏi về nơi ở trong quá khứ → Where did + S + V nguyên mẫu

exercises.q: Don't be late, ___ you?
exercises.exp: Câu mệnh lệnh phủ định (Don't...) → đuôi: will you

exercises.q: ___ (Ai) told you about the surprise party? (subject question)
exercises.exp: Subject question: who = chủ ngữ → chỉ cần 'Who told you'

exercises.q: ___ (Ai mà) you were talking to just now?
exercises.exp: Object question: you = S, who = tân ngữ của 'talking to' → Who were you talking to?

exercises.q: ___ (Cái gì) broke the window? (subject question, past)
exercises.exp: What + động từ + ... → câu hỏi về chủ ngữ, không đảo ngữ

exercises.q: You haven't told her yet, ___ (đuôi câu hỏi)?
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định 'haven't' → đuôi câu hỏi dùng have you (khẳng định)

exercises.q: ___ (Ai đã gửi) this package to the office? (subject question)
exercises.exp: Who + V (past) → câu hỏi chủ ngữ, không dùng did

exercises.q: We should leave now, ___ (đuôi)?
exercises.exp: should (khẳng định) → đuôi câu hỏi: shouldn't we

exercises.q: ___ (Điều gì đã xảy ra) to make her so upset? (subject question)
exercises.exp: What + happened → câu hỏi chủ ngữ, không dùng did

exercises.q: She's never been to Paris, ___ (đuôi câu hỏi)?
exercises.exp: Câu phủ định (has never been) → đuôi khẳng định: has she

exercises.q: Something ___ (happened) to the computer? (subject question với 'what')
exercises.exp: What = chủ ngữ → What happened? (không dùng did)

exercises.q: ___ (Điều gì) did she say to upset you so much?
exercises.exp: Object question: she = S, what = tân ngữ → What did she say

exercises.q: I am right about this, ___ (đuôi câu hỏi)?
exercises.exp: I am → đuôi đặc biệt: aren't I (không dùng amn't I)

exercises.q: ___ (Đội nào) won the championship? (subject question)
exercises.exp: Which team = chủ ngữ → Which team won? (không dùng did)

exercises.q: Nobody saw anything suspicious, ___ (đuôi)?
exercises.exp: Nobody = phủ định → đuôi khẳng định: did they

exercises.q: ___ (Tại sao) she crying? Is she okay?
exercises.exp: Hỏi lý do ở hiện tại → Why is she crying?

exercises.q: Let's start the meeting now, ___ (đuôi)?
exercises.exp: Let's... → đuôi đặc biệt: shall we

exercises.q: ___ (Ai) you spoke with at the airport? (object question, past)
exercises.exp: Object question: you = S → Who did you speak with?

exercises.q: You've already submitted the form, ___ (đuôi)?
exercises.exp: Câu khẳng định (have submitted) → đuôi phủ định: haven't you

exercises.q: ___ (Điều gì) makes you say that? (subject question, present)
exercises.exp: What = chủ ngữ → What makes you say that? (không dùng does)

exercises.q: There isn't enough time, ___ (đuôi)?
exercises.exp: There isn't (phủ định) → đuôi khẳng định: is there

exercises.q: ___ (Chưa học xong à) you finished reading the report? (negative question, surprise)
exercises.exp: Negative question to express surprise → Haven't you finished...?

passage.text: Sarah was nervous before the job interview. She kept asking herself: '{{1}} will they ask me?' and '{{2}} will the interviewer be like?' She arrived early — the building was impressive. '{{3}} been here before?' her friend asked. 'No, never,' she replied. There was another candidate in the waiting room. '{{4}} long have you been waiting?' Sarah asked politely. 'About ten minutes,' he said. '{{5}} applied for the marketing position?' Sarah asked, curious. 'No, the design role,' he replied. '{{6}} takes the longest in interviews?' she wondered to herself. Finally, she was called in. The interviewer smiled. '{{7}} makes you the right person for this role?' he asked. Sarah took a deep breath. She answered confidently — it was the question she had prepared for, {{8}} it?

The school inspector visited Class 10B on a Tuesday morning. '{{1}} designed this project?' she asked, looking impressed at the display on the wall. 'We all did,' said the class representative. '{{2}} long did it take you?' the inspector continued. 'About three weeks,' the student replied. The inspector turned to the teacher: 'You've been working here for quite some time, {{3}}?' The teacher smiled. 'Yes, twelve years now.' '{{4}} teaches the most advanced group?' asked the inspector. 'Mrs Patel does,' said the teacher. 'And {{5}} did you choose this particular topic?' The class fell silent. Then one student raised her hand. 'We chose it {{6}} it connects to real life,' she explained. The inspector nodded. 'You haven't covered the second unit yet, {{7}}?' she asked. 'Not yet,' admitted the teacher. 'But {{8}} is stopping you?' 'Time,' he said simply. '{{9}} of the students find the workload too heavy?' she asked. 'A few do,' he replied honestly. The inspector smiled. 'This is one of the best classrooms I've seen. You should be proud, {{10}}?'

At the community centre, a journalist was interviewing local residents about changes in the neighbourhood. '{{1}} noticed the new building first?' she began. 'I did,' said an elderly man. '{{2}} did you think when you saw it?' 'I was shocked,' he admitted. '{{3}} has been in charge of the planning committee?' the journalist asked a second resident. 'Councillor Davis has,' she replied. 'You've lived here a long time, {{4}}?' said the journalist. 'Over forty years,' the woman confirmed. '{{5}} do you think caused all these changes?' 'Money,' said the man. The journalist turned to a younger resident. '{{6}} involved you in the decision-making process?' she asked. 'Nobody did,' he said, frustrated. 'Don't you think that's unfair, {{7}}?' The young man agreed strongly. '{{8}} meetings have been held about this?' she continued. 'Only two,' said the councillor nearby. 'And {{9}} attended those meetings?' 'Mainly council members,' he admitted quietly. The journalist closed her notebook. 'There's clearly more to investigate here, {{10}}?'

Mr Collins was a detective known for asking the right questions. He arrived at the scene and immediately began. '{{1}} discovered the missing documents?' he asked the secretary. 'I did, at 8 a.m.,' she said. '{{2}} were you doing before that?' he continued. 'Making coffee,' she replied nervously. '{{3}} has access to this room?' he asked the manager. 'Only senior staff,' the manager answered. 'There are only three senior staff members, {{4}}?' 'That's correct,' said the manager. '{{5}} authorised the changes to the filing system last month?' The manager hesitated. 'Ms Greene did.' '{{6}} gave her that authority?' said Mr Collins sharply. 'I did,' admitted the manager. He turned to a junior employee standing nearby. 'You were here on Saturday, {{7}}?' The employee nodded. '{{8}} else was here?' 'Just the cleaner,' the employee said. '{{9}} let the cleaner in?' '{{10}} always does — it's part of the routine,' said the secretary.

It was the first day of a new job and Elena had many questions for her colleague, David. '{{1}} is responsible for approving our expenses?' she asked. 'The finance team is,' said David. '{{2}} do we usually submit reports — on Fridays?' 'Yes, that's right,' he confirmed. 'We have access to the shared drive, {{3}}?' Elena asked. 'Yes, you should have got the link by email,' David replied. '{{4}} set up the internal communication system here?' she continued. 'The IT department did, about two years ago,' he said. '{{5}} made the decision to switch to this software?' 'The CEO did,' said David. 'You've been trained to use it already, {{6}}?' 'Yes, briefly,' Elena replied. '{{7}} do I contact if I have a technical issue?' 'Just email support,' he said. '{{8}} departments use the same system?' Elena asked. 'All of them do,' said David. 'There are no exceptions, {{9}}?' 'None whatsoever,' he confirmed. '{{10}} should I speak to about my contract details?'

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một cuộc phỏng vấn xin việc hoặc một cuộc trò chuyện quan trọng — bạn đã được hỏi những câu hỏi gì, bạn đã hỏi lại điều gì, và kết quả ra sao (sử dụng nhiều dạng câu hỏi khác nhau)
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Direct questions, subject questions, negative questions, tag questions
writing_prompts.example: My job interview last spring was one of the most nerve-racking experiences of my life. I had prepared well — or so I thought. The interviewer started with a simple question: 'What made you apply for this position?' followed immediately by 'Haven't you applied here before?' which caught me off guard. I answered as calmly as I could. Then she asked me about a difficult situation I had managed. 'Who took responsibility when the project failed?' she asked pointedly. I was honest and said it had been a shared failure. Near the end, I asked her a question of my own: 'What does a typical day look like in this role?' She seemed impressed. 'You're not just interested in the salary, are you?' she said with a smile. I left the building not knowing whether I had performed well. The email arrived two days later — I had got the job. It was the best news I had received in years, wasn't it?

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một cuộc điều tra hoặc tình huống giải quyết vấn đề — ai đã đặt câu hỏi, họ hỏi gì, và kết quả ra sao. Sử dụng nhiều loại câu hỏi khác nhau: câu hỏi chủ ngữ, câu hỏi đuôi, câu hỏi phủ định.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Subject questions, tag questions, negative questions
writing_prompts.example: Last year, a valuable painting disappeared from the local museum, and the investigation that followed was fascinating. Who discovered it was missing? A security guard noticed the empty frame at 7 a.m. on a Monday morning. The police arrived quickly and began asking questions. 'You were here on Sunday evening, weren't you?' an officer asked the night guard. 'Yes, I was,' he replied nervously. 'Who let the last visitors out?' 'I did,' he said. 'And didn't you check the gallery before locking up?' The guard admitted he had been distracted. What happened next was unexpected. A cleaner came forward and said she had seen someone acting strangely near the painting on Sunday afternoon. 'Who was it?' the detective asked. 'I'm not sure,' she said, 'but hasn't anyone checked the CCTV?' They had not. The footage revealed everything. It was a member of staff who had been dismissed the previous month. The case was closed within a week.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một buổi học hoặc lớp học mà bạn thấy thú vị — giáo viên đã đặt những câu hỏi nào, học sinh phản hồi ra sao, và điều gì khiến buổi học đáng nhớ. Dùng câu hỏi chủ ngữ, câu hỏi đuôi và câu hỏi thể phủ định.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Different question types in a classroom context
writing_prompts.example: The most memorable class I have ever attended was a history lesson about the causes of the First World War. The teacher did not lecture at all — instead, she asked questions from the very beginning. 'Who can tell me what triggered the conflict?' she began. Several hands went up immediately. 'What caused the alliance system to break down so quickly?' she continued. One student gave a thoughtful answer about nationalism. 'Isn't that exactly what we discussed last week?' the teacher said, smiling. 'You've all read the chapter on alliances, haven't you?' Most students nodded, though a few looked at the floor. 'Didn't anyone find it surprising that a single assassination could lead to a world war?' This sparked a lively debate. Who agreed with the student's interpretation? Who challenged it? The room was buzzing. What made this lesson so effective was that the teacher treated us as equals. By the end, we had formed our own views — not simply repeated what a textbook said.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một lần hiểu lầm hoặc tình huống giao tiếp bị nhầm lẫn — bạn hoặc người khác đã đặt câu hỏi sai, câu hỏi không rõ ràng, hoặc không hiểu đúng ý nhau. Dùng nhiều loại câu hỏi và câu hỏi đuôi.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Subject questions, tag questions, negative questions in narrative
writing_prompts.example: A few months ago, a simple misunderstanding at work led to a rather embarrassing situation. My manager had sent a message asking me to prepare a report on client feedback. I assumed she wanted it by the end of the week — but didn't she say 'by Thursday'? Apparently, she had. On Thursday morning, she came to my desk. 'You've finished the report, haven't you?' she asked. 'Not quite,' I admitted. 'Who told you the deadline was Friday?' she asked, visibly frustrated. 'Nobody did — I just assumed,' I said. 'Didn't you check the email?' 'I did, but I misread it.' Who was responsible for the confusion? Honestly, both of us. She hadn't been specific enough, and I hadn't asked for clarification. 'Why didn't you come and ask me if you weren't sure?' she said, and she was right. What happened next was that she gave me an extra two hours. I finished it just in time. Since then, I always confirm deadlines — isn't that the sensible thing to do?

writing_prompts.vi: Tưởng tượng bạn là phóng viên và phỏng vấn một nhân vật nổi tiếng. Mô tả cuộc phỏng vấn đó — bạn hỏi gì, họ trả lời ra sao, và bạn đã phát hiện ra điều gì bất ngờ (dùng đa dạng các loại câu hỏi)
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: All question types: WH-, subject questions, negative questions, tag questions
writing_prompts.example: I had always dreamed of interviewing a world-famous musician, and last year that dream became a reality. We met in a quiet café in London. 'Who first inspired you to play the piano?' I asked. She smiled and mentioned her grandmother, who played every Sunday. 'What made you choose this career over a more stable path?' I continued. She laughed. 'Didn't anyone ever tell you that music is one of the most unstable careers in the world?' she replied. 'They did,' I said, 'but that didn't stop you, did it?' She agreed. Then she surprised me: 'Who do you think writes my songs?' she asked. I assumed she wrote them herself — hadn't she always claimed that? But she revealed that her younger sister co-wrote almost everything. What a revelation! Nobody had ever reported that before, had they?

upper_intermediate.title: Indirect Questions
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 Indirect Questions là gì?
Câu hỏi gián tiếp (indirect questions) lịch sự và trang trọng hơn câu hỏi trực tiếp. Chúng thường bắt đầu bằng:
Can/Could you tell me…? / Do you know…? / Would you mind telling me…? / I wonder… / I'm not sure… / I'd like to know…

⚠️ Quy tắc then chốt: Trong câu hỏi gián tiếp, trật tự từ là S + V (như câu khẳng định), KHÔNG đảo ngược như câu hỏi thông thường.
📌 Cấu trúc – WH- Questions (Câu hỏi có từ hỏi)
Câu hỏi trực tiếpCâu hỏi gián tiếp
Where is the bank?Could you tell me where the bank is?
What does she do?Do you know what she does?
When did they arrive?I wonder when they arrived.
What is he doing here?I don't know what he is doing here.
Where has she been?I'm not sure where she has been.

✦ Lưu ý: Bỏ do/does/did, giữ nguyên thì của động từ chính.
📌 Cấu trúc – Yes/No Questions (Câu hỏi đúng/sai)
Thêm if / whether vào giữa:
Câu hỏi trực tiếpCâu hỏi gián tiếp
Is she coming?Do you know if/whether she is coming?
Did he call?I'm not sure if/whether he called.
Can she drive?Could you tell me whether she can drive?
⚠️ Những lỗi thường gặp
❌ Sai✅ Đúng
Can you tell me where is the station?Can you tell me where the station is?
Do you know what does she want?Do you know what she wants?
I wonder did he arrive yet.I wonder if he arrived / if he has arrived.
Could you tell me when did she leave?Could you tell me when she left?
Could you tell me where the nearest pharmacy is?Gián tiếp: S+V trật tự thông thường, không đảo ngược
I'd like to know whether the meeting has been cancelled.Yes/No → dùng if hoặc whether
I'm not sure when she will finish the report.Không dùng 'when will she finish' trong câu gián tiếp

exercises.q: Could you tell me where ___ the post office?
exercises.opts: the post office is,is the post office,does the post office,the post office does
exercises.exp: Câu hỏi gián tiếp: S + V (the post office is), không đảo ngược

exercises.q: Do you know ___ she will be back?
exercises.exp: Gián tiếp: when + S + V (when she will be back)

exercises.q: I'm not sure ___ the meeting has been cancelled.
exercises.exp: Yes/No question gián tiếp → dùng if (hoặc whether)

exercises.q: Do you know what time ___ tomorrow?
exercises.opts: the train leaves,does the train leave,will the train leave,leaves the train
exercises.exp: Câu hỏi gián tiếp: what time + S + V (the train leaves)

exercises.q: Could you tell me ___ the library is open on Sundays?
exercises.exp: Yes/No gián tiếp → whether (hoặc if)

exercises.q: I wonder ___ she is feeling better now.
exercises.opts: if,that,whether or not,both A and C
exercises.exp: I wonder + if/whether → cả hai đều đúng cho Yes/No gián tiếp

exercises.q: Do you know ___ the director arrived?
exercises.exp: Gián tiếp: when + S + V nguyên mẫu không đảo (when the director arrived)

exercises.q: Can you tell me ___ to get to the city centre from here?
exercises.opts: how,how do I,how can I,which way do
exercises.exp: Gián tiếp với how: Can you tell me how to get... (how + to infinitive rút gọn)

exercises.q: I'd like to know ___ you made that decision.
exercises.exp: Gián tiếp: why + S + V (why you made...); answer A maps to 'why' before S+V

exercises.q: Could you tell me ___ is in charge of the project?
exercises.exp: Subject question gián tiếp: who = chủ ngữ → who is in charge (giữ nguyên trật tự)

exercises.q: I'm not sure ___ she has finished her dissertation.
exercises.exp: Yes/No gián tiếp → whether (hoặc if)

exercises.q: Do you have any idea ___ the results will be announced?
exercises.exp: Câu hỏi gián tiếp: when + S + V (when the results will be announced)

exercises.q: Could you explain ___ you reached this conclusion?
exercises.exp: Gián tiếp: how + S + V (how you reached...)

exercises.q: I wonder ___ anyone noticed what happened.
exercises.exp: Yes/No gián tiếp → if

exercises.q: Would you mind telling me ___ platform the train departs from?
exercises.exp: which platform → câu hỏi lựa chọn gián tiếp (which + noun + S + V)

exercises.q: Do you know ___ she called yesterday?
exercises.exp: Gián tiếp: why + S + V (why she called)

exercises.q: I'm not sure ___ I should accept the offer.
exercises.exp: Yes/No gián tiếp → whether (= if)

exercises.q: Could you tell me ___ the CEO's name is?
exercises.exp: Gián tiếp: what + S + V (what the CEO's name is)

exercises.q: Do you have any idea ___ the project will be completed?
exercises.exp: Gián tiếp: when + S + V (when the project will be completed)

exercises.q: I'd like to know ___ you have ever visited Vietnam.
exercises.exp: Yes/No gián tiếp → whether (hoặc if)

exercises.q: Could you tell me where ___ (the nearest bus stop / là)?
exercises.exp: Câu hỏi gián tiếp: S+V, không đảo ngược → 'where the nearest bus stop is'

exercises.q: She asked me ___ (tôi có biết không) where the nearest hospital was.
exercises.ans: if I knew / whether I knew
exercises.exp: if/whether + S + V (không đảo ngữ) → câu hỏi gián tiếp dạng Yes/No

exercises.q: Could you let me know ___ (khi nào) the parcel will be delivered?
exercises.exp: when + S + V → câu hỏi gián tiếp về thời gian, không đảo ngữ

exercises.q: I'd like to know ___ (ai đã quyết định) to change the schedule.
exercises.exp: who + V → câu hỏi gián tiếp chủ ngữ, không dùng did

exercises.q: Do you have any idea ___ (bao nhiêu người) attended the conference?
exercises.exp: how many + noun + S + V → câu hỏi gián tiếp về số lượng

exercises.q: I'm not certain ___ (liệu có) any seats left for tonight's show.
exercises.ans: whether there are / if there are
exercises.exp: whether/if + there are → câu hỏi gián tiếp Yes/No về sự tồn tại

exercises.q: Can you explain ___ (tại sao hệ thống bị) down for so long yesterday?
exercises.exp: why + S + V (past) → câu hỏi gián tiếp về lý do, không đảo ngữ

exercises.q: Do you know what time ___ (the film / bắt đầu)?
exercises.exp: Gián tiếp: what time + S + V (bỏ does)

exercises.q: I'm not sure ___ (liệu) she has received my email.
exercises.exp: Yes/No gián tiếp → if hoặc whether

exercises.q: Could you tell me why ___ (anh ấy / nghỉ việc) last month?
exercises.exp: Gián tiếp: why + S + V nguyên mẫu (bỏ did)

exercises.q: I wonder ___ (ai đã thiết kế) this building. It's beautiful.
exercises.exp: Subject question gián tiếp: who + V (không đảo, không thêm did)

exercises.q: Do you have any idea ___ (bao lâu) the journey takes?
exercises.exp: Gián tiếp: how long + S + V (how long the journey takes)

exercises.q: Would you mind telling me ___ (phòng họp / ở đâu)?
exercises.exp: Câu hỏi gián tiếp: where + S + V (không đảo)

exercises.q: I'd like to know ___ (liệu) the tickets are still available.
exercises.exp: Yes/No gián tiếp → if/whether + S + V

exercises.q: Can you tell me what ___ (cô ấy / đang làm) in the office at midnight?
exercises.exp: Gián tiếp: what + S + V (what she was doing)

exercises.q: I wonder ___ (khi nào) the new manager will arrive.
exercises.exp: Câu hỏi gián tiếp: when + S + V (when the new manager will arrive)

exercises.q: Could you explain ___ (tại sao) the flight was delayed?
exercises.exp: Gián tiếp: why + S + V (bỏ was đảo → giữ nguyên)

exercises.q: Do you know ___ (ai) is responsible for this department?
exercises.exp: Subject question gián tiếp: who + V (who is responsible)

exercises.q: I'm not sure ___ (có nên) I accept the invitation or not.
exercises.exp: whether...or not → dùng whether (không dùng if...or not thông thường)

exercises.q: Can you tell me ___ (tòa nhà này được xây / khi nào)?

passage.text: Anna was new to the city and had many questions. First, she asked a passer-by if {{1}} a pharmacy nearby. The person wasn't sure, so Anna went into a café and asked the barista whether {{2}} a local map available. The barista handed her one and asked where {{3}}. Anna explained she was looking for the train station. He said he wasn't sure exactly which platform {{4}} from, but he could tell her roughly where {{5}}. Anna thanked him and left. On the street, she stopped a police officer and asked him how long {{6}} on foot. She also wanted to know if {{7}} any lockers at the station for her luggage. The officer replied that he didn't know whether {{8}} lockers on the main concourse or the side entrance. He suggested she ask at the information desk, where they could tell her {{9}} the next train to the airport {{10}}.

Ben had just started university and felt overwhelmed. He went to the student services office to ask for help. First, he wanted to know {{1}} he could change his module choices. The receptionist said she wasn't sure {{2}} the deadline for changes had already passed. She suggested he ask the academic advisor, who explained {{3}} he could make changes until the end of the first week. Ben then asked {{4}} he needed to fill in a form or just send an email. 'Just email us,' said the advisor. Ben also wondered {{5}} there was a support group for first-year students. The advisor told him {{6}} several groups met on campus each week. He asked her {{7}} the next meeting was. She checked her screen and said {{8}} it was the following Thursday at 5 p.m. Ben asked {{9}} he could bring a friend along. 'Of course,' she smiled. Finally, he wanted to know {{10}} the meeting would be held.

The customer called the airline to ask about her delayed flight. First, she asked {{1}} the flight had been cancelled or just postponed. The agent said he couldn't confirm {{2}} a new departure time had been set yet. She asked him {{3}} the delay was due to weather or a technical issue. He said he wasn't sure {{4}} he could share that information publicly. She then asked {{5}} she would be entitled to compensation. He explained {{6}} passengers on flights delayed over three hours were eligible. She wanted to know {{7}} she needed to apply online or in person. He said {{8}} the process was entirely online. She then asked {{9}} her connecting flight would still be held. He admitted he didn't know {{10}} the connecting airline had been informed of the delay.

A journalist was researching a story about local food markets. She called several stallholders to gather information. First, she asked one trader {{1}} he had been selling at the market for a long time. He said {{2}} he had been there for over fifteen years. She asked him {{3}} the market was open every day. He explained {{4}} it only operated on weekends. She then asked {{5}} the council had ever tried to close it down. He said he wasn't certain {{6}} there had been any official discussions about it. She asked {{7}} the market attracted many visitors in summer. 'Absolutely,' he said, adding {{8}} visitor numbers had doubled in recent years. She wondered {{9}} the stalls were all locally owned. He confirmed {{10}} the vast majority were, with just a few exceptions.

Maya was applying for a research grant and had many questions for the funding body. She called and asked {{1}} the application deadline was. The assistant told her {{2}} all applications had to be submitted by the 30th. She asked {{3}} she could apply as an independent researcher or only through a university. He said {{4}} both options were acceptable. She then asked {{5}} the review process took long. He replied {{6}} it usually took around eight weeks. She wanted to know {{7}} successful applicants were notified by email or letter. He confirmed {{8}} they used email for all communications. She asked {{9}} she could speak to a previous grant recipient for advice. He said {{10}} he could share a list of past recipients who had agreed to be contacted.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một lần bạn phải hỏi đường hoặc tìm kiếm thông tin ở một nơi xa lạ — bạn đã hỏi những câu gì, mọi người đã trả lời ra sao, và bạn có tìm được thứ mình cần không (dùng câu hỏi gián tiếp)
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Indirect questions: could you tell me, do you know, I wonder, I'm not sure, whether, if
writing_prompts.example: Last summer, I found myself completely lost in a city I had never visited before. My phone had died, and I had no way to check where I was. I stopped a shopkeeper and politely asked whether she could tell me where the central train station was. She wasn't entirely sure, so she asked a colleague if he knew which street the station was on. He explained that he wasn't certain whether the station was undergoing construction or not, but he thought it was about fifteen minutes' walk away. I then asked a young woman whether she knew how long the walk would take and if there were any taxis nearby. She told me she wasn't sure whether the taxi rank was still operating on that side of the road. Eventually, I found a tourist information booth and asked the assistant what time the last train to my destination departed. To my enormous relief, I had forty minutes to spare.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một lần bạn phải tìm kiếm thông tin quan trọng — bạn đã hỏi ai, hỏi bằng cách nào, và kết quả ra sao. Dùng câu hỏi gián tiếp để thuật lại những câu hỏi bạn đã đặt ra.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Indirect questions with whether, if, what, when, where, who, how
hint_words: I had no idea how long
writing_prompts.example: Two years ago, I had to find out whether I was eligible for a government training scheme. I called the relevant office and asked whether the programme was open to people who were already employed part-time. The person I spoke to wasn't sure if my situation qualified, so she transferred me to a specialist. I asked her how long I would need to wait for an answer. She told me it could take up to ten working days. I also wanted to know whether I needed to submit supporting documents at the same time as the application, or afterwards. She explained that everything had to be sent together. I then asked who would be reviewing my application and whether there was any way to check its progress online. She confirmed that there was a tracking system and gave me a reference number. A week later, I received an email saying I had been accepted. I was so relieved — I had spent weeks worrying about whether it would work out.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy viết về một cuộc trò chuyện hoặc cuộc họp trong đó bạn phải đặt nhiều câu hỏi gián tiếp — ví dụ như tại một hội nghị, buổi phỏng vấn, hay cuộc gặp với chuyên gia. Mô tả chi tiết bạn đã hỏi gì và phản hồi bạn nhận được.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Indirect questions in formal/semi-formal contexts
writing_prompts.example: Last spring, I attended a careers fair where I had the chance to speak directly with representatives from several companies. I had prepared a list of questions, but I was careful to phrase them politely using indirect forms. At the first stand, I asked whether the company offered graduate training programmes and whether applications were open yet. The representative told me that they were, and explained what the selection process involved. I then asked if she could tell me how long the programme typically lasted and whether participants were paid a full salary from the start. She answered both questions clearly. At a second company, I wanted to know whether there were opportunities to work internationally, and if so, whether employees chose their location or were assigned one. The recruiter said he wasn't sure whether that information was publicly available, but took my email address. I also asked a third company whether they considered candidates without a specific degree background. By the end of the day, I had learned far more than I expected — and I had done it all without once asking a direct question.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một nhân vật (thật hoặc tưởng tượng) đang đặt câu hỏi về cuộc sống của mình — điều họ muốn biết về tương lai, về mối quan hệ của họ, hoặc về những quyết định họ đã đưa ra. Dùng câu hỏi gián tiếp và ngôn ngữ nội tâm.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Indirect questions for introspection and reported thought
hint_words: She had no idea how long
writing_prompts.example: Clara sat by the window and stared at the rain. She wondered whether she had made the right decision by turning down the promotion. She asked herself if the extra money would really have made her happier, or whether she had simply been afraid of the extra responsibility. She had no idea how long she would stay in her current role, or whether a better opportunity would come along. She couldn't tell whether her colleagues had been surprised by her choice, or whether they had understood. She wanted to know if her manager still respected her, since the conversation after her refusal had been awkward and brief. Most of all, she wondered whether things would ever feel settled again. She wasn't sure what the right answer was — only that she had acted honestly, and that perhaps that was enough. She finally asked herself whether she could learn to stop second-guessing every decision she made, and decided, quietly, that she could try.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một đoạn hội thoại hoặc kể lại một tình huống ở nơi làm việc hoặc trường học, khi bạn cần hỏi thông tin một cách lịch sự từ cấp trên hoặc người lạ — sử dụng câu hỏi gián tiếp nhiều nhất có thể
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Polite indirect questions in formal/semi-formal contexts
writing_prompts.example: When I joined my new company, I quickly learnt that being polite was essential in all professional communication. On my first day, I needed to find out a great deal of information but did not want to seem demanding. I approached the office manager and asked whether she could tell me where the nearest printer was and if there was a keycode for the stationery cupboard. She told me she wasn't sure whether the printer on the third floor was working or not. I also asked a colleague if he knew what time the team meeting started on Mondays. He said he wasn't certain whether it had been moved to Tuesday recently. I sent a message to my supervisor asking if she could let me know when I would receive my access card and whether or not I needed to attend the induction on Friday. By the end of my first week, I had learnt that asking questions indirectly was not only more polite — it also seemed to get better results.

upper_intermediate.title: Position of Adverbs and Adverb Phrases
upper_intermediate.content:
📌 Ba vị trí của trạng từ (Adverb Positions)
Trạng từ và cụm trạng từ có thể đứng ở ba vị trí:
① Initial position – Đầu câu (trước chủ ngữ)
Yesterday, I went to the library. / Suddenly, the alarm went off.

② Mid position – Giữa câu (trước động từ chính)
She often calls me late at night. / They don't always agree.

③ Final position – Cuối câu (sau tân ngữ hoặc sau động từ)
I don't understand you when you speak quickly. / She dances beautifully.
📌 Mid Position – Chi tiết
Mid position = trước động từ chính
✦ Sau động từ be (khi là động từ chính): They are often late.
✦ Sau trợ động từ đầu tiên: You must never do that. / I have often been tempted.
✦ Trong câu hỏi: sau chủ ngữ: Have you ever been to Rome?

Trạng từ tần suất (frequency adverbs) thường ở mid position:
always, usually, often, sometimes, occasionally, rarely, seldom, never
⚠️ sometimes, usually, normally cũng có thể đứng đầu câu.
Sometimes I feel a bit lost. / I sometimes feel a bit lost.
📌 Trạng từ cách thức (Adverbs of Manner)
Vị trí phổ biến nhất: final position
She can dance salsa marvellously. / He works hard.

Mid position: thường chỉ trong văn học / thể bị động
He carefully took the flower. (văn học)
The driver was seriously injured. (bị động)

⚠️ well, badly, hard, fast KHÔNG thể đứng mid position:
❌ She well speaks English. → ✅ She speaks English well.
📌 Trạng từ thời gian & Trạng từ quan điểm
Adverbs of time (yesterday, soon, recently, lately...): thường ở final hoặc initial:
They'll be here soon. / Yesterday, I met an old friend.

Viewpoint / comment adverbs (obviously, fortunately, apparently, luckily, surprisingly...): thường ở initial hoặc mid:
Obviously, there's been a misunderstanding. / She clearly didn't enjoy the meal.

Adverbs of degree (completely, nearly, almost, absolutely, slightly...): thường ở mid:
I completely forgot about the meeting. / She almost missed the flight.
⚠️ Trật tự khi nhiều trạng từ cùng xuất hiện
Khi có nhiều trạng từ cuối câu, trật tự thông thường là:
Manner → Place → Time
She worked quietly (manner) in her office (place) all morning (time).
He drove carefully through the city centre last night.
I completely forgot to call her. It was a terrible mistake.Degree adverb → mid position (trước động từ chính)
Fortunately, nobody was injured in the accident.Comment adverb → initial position, theo sau là dấu phẩy
She played the piece flawlessly on stage last night.Manner → Place → Time ở cuối câu

exercises.q: She ___ wakes up before six. She's an early riser.
exercises.exp: Frequency adverb 'always' → mid position: trước động từ chính 'wakes'

exercises.q: He speaks very ___. I can't understand him.
exercises.exp: Manner adverb → final position sau động từ

exercises.q: ___, the rain stopped just before the outdoor ceremony.
exercises.exp: Comment adverb → initial position, theo sau là dấy phẩy

exercises.q: You must ___ leave your belongings unattended in the airport.
exercises.exp: Frequency adverb 'never' → mid position: sau modal (must)

exercises.q: I have ___ been to New Zealand. I'd love to go one day.
exercises.exp: never → mid position: sau trợ động từ đầu tiên 'have'

exercises.q: She ___ late for work. It's becoming a real problem.
exercises.opts: is always,always is,always be,is always being
exercises.exp: Be là động từ chính → frequency adverb đứng SAU be: 'is always'

exercises.q: The patient was ___ injured in the car accident.
exercises.exp: Adverb of manner → mid position với bị động (was seriously injured)

exercises.q: I ___ forgot about the appointment. I'm so sorry.
exercises.exp: Degree adverb 'completely' → mid position: trước V chính (forgot)

exercises.q: She sang the song ___ in the concert hall last evening.
exercises.exp: Adverb of manner → final position (beautifully in the concert hall last evening)

exercises.q: ___, he arrived just as we were about to give up on him.
exercises.exp: Time adverb 'eventually' → initial position để nhấn mạnh

exercises.q: She ___ finishes her work on time. The manager is very happy.
exercises.exp: Frequency adverb 'always' → mid position trước V chính 'finishes'

exercises.q: He has ___ considered changing careers.
exercises.exp: often → mid position: sau trợ động từ đầu tiên 'has' (has often considered)

exercises.q: The project was ___ completed by the end of the month.
exercises.exp: Adverb 'finally' → mid position với bị động (was finally completed)

exercises.q: She ___ forgets names, even of people she's known for years.
exercises.exp: Frequency adverb 'often' → mid position trước V chính 'forgets'

exercises.q: ___, she had no idea what had happened behind the scenes.
exercises.exp: Comment adverb 'apparently' → initial position, theo sau là dấu phẩy

exercises.q: I ___ imagined I would live in another country.
exercises.exp: never → mid position trước V chính (never imagined)

exercises.q: She speaks English very ___. She studied abroad for three years.
exercises.exp: 'well' = adverb của 'good'; 'well' không thể đứng mid position

exercises.q: He ___ gets nervous before presentations. He's very confident.
exercises.exp: Frequency adverb 'rarely' → mid position trước V chính

exercises.q: They worked ___ in the new office ___ all week.
exercises.exp: 'hard' là adverb (hard worker = badly → hard ≠ hardly); downtown = adverb of place

exercises.q: ___, the company had to close three of its branches.
exercises.exp: Comment adverb 'unfortunately' → initial position, theo sau là dấu phẩy

exercises.q: I ___ (chưa bao giờ) tried skydiving. It sounds terrifying.
exercises.exp: 'never' → mid position: sau trợ động từ (have never tried)

exercises.q: She ___ (đột nhiên) burst into tears during the presentation.
exercises.exp: suddenly → trạng từ chỉ cách thức/thời điểm, đứng trước động từ chính hoặc đầu câu

exercises.q: ___ (Ngay lập tức), the firefighters rushed into the burning building.
exercises.exp: Immediately → trạng từ thời gian, có thể đứng đầu câu để nhấn mạnh

exercises.q: He ___ (vẫn chưa) finished editing the document when the meeting started.
exercises.exp: still + hadn't / hadn't + yet → diễn đạt việc chưa hoàn thành tại thời điểm quá khứ

exercises.q: She speaks French ___ (khá tốt) for someone who has only studied it for a year.
exercises.exp: quite/fairly + well → trạng từ mức độ + trạng từ cách thức, đứng sau động từ

exercises.q: ___ (Đáng chú ý thay), the youngest competitor won the entire championship.
exercises.exp: Remarkably/Surprisingly → trạng từ bình luận (comment adverb), thường đứng đầu câu, phân cách bởi dấu phẩy

exercises.q: She ___ (thường xuyên) arrives late. Her colleagues are frustrated.
exercises.exp: Frequency adverb → mid position trước V chính (often arrives)

exercises.q: ___ (May mắn thay), nobody was hurt in the accident.
exercises.exp: Comment adverb → initial position, theo sau là dấu phẩy

exercises.q: He works very ___ (chăm chỉ). He deserves a promotion.
exercises.exp: 'hard' là adverb cách thức → final position (không phải 'hardly')

exercises.q: She ___ (hoàn toàn) forgot to submit the report.
exercises.exp: Degree adverb → mid position trước V chính (completely forgot)

exercises.q: He is ___ (luôn luôn) late. It drives me crazy.
exercises.exp: Be là V chính → adverb đứng SAU be (is always)

exercises.q: The presentation was ___ (rõ ràng) a great success.
exercises.exp: Comment/viewpoint adverb → mid position hoặc initial position

exercises.q: You must ___ (không được bao giờ) share your password with anyone.
exercises.exp: 'never' → sau modal verb (must never)

exercises.q: She ___ (hầu như) missed her flight because of the traffic.
exercises.exp: Degree adverb 'almost' → mid position trước V chính (almost missed)

exercises.q: He ___ (hiếm khi) goes out at night since he started the new job.
exercises.exp: Frequency adverb → mid position trước V chính

exercises.q: She played the violin ___ (hay) ___ (trên sân khấu) ___ (tối qua).
exercises.exp: Manner → Place → Time: thứ tự cuối câu

exercises.q: ___ (Rõ ràng là), there has been a serious misunderstanding between them.
exercises.exp: Comment adverb → initial position, theo sau là dấu phẩy

exercises.q: I have ___ (thỉnh thoảng) thought about moving abroad.
exercises.exp: 'sometimes' → mid position sau trợ động từ (have sometimes thought)

exercises.q: She ___ (hầu như luôn) finishes her work before anyone else.
exercises.exp: Degree + frequency ('almost always') → mid position trước V chính

exercises.q: The patient was ___ (nghiêm trọng) injured and taken to hospital.
exercises.exp: Manner adverb trong câu bị động → mid position (was seriously injured)

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về thói quen hàng ngày của một người bạn biết — họ thường làm gì, hiếm khi làm gì, và điều gì gần đây đã thay đổi trong cuộc sống của họ (sử dụng nhiều trạng từ ở đúng vị trí)
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Position of adverbs: frequency (always, often, rarely, never), degree (completely, almost), comment (fortunately, apparently), manner (carefully, quietly)
writing_prompts.example: My friend Linh is one of the most disciplined people I have ever met. She always wakes up before five in the morning and rarely, if ever, stays out late. She has recently started running every day and has completely transformed her lifestyle over the past six months. She works extremely hard at her job, yet she never complains about long hours. Apparently, she was quite different a few years ago — she frequently overslept and often skipped breakfast. Fortunately, a health scare prompted her to change. Now she carefully plans her meals every Sunday and almost never eats processed food. She has already completed two half-marathons this year and occasionally talks about entering a full marathon next spring. Interestingly, despite working longer hours than ever, she says she has never felt more energetic or focused.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy kể lại một tình huống khẩn cấp hoặc căng thẳng mà bạn đã từng chứng kiến hoặc trải qua — mô tả mọi người đã phản ứng như thế nào, nhanh hay chậm, bình tĩnh hay hoảng loạn. Sử dụng trạng từ chỉ thời gian, cách thức và thái độ đa dạng.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Position of adverbs of manner, time, frequency, and comment adverbs
writing_prompts.example: A few years ago, I witnessed a minor accident at a busy intersection near my workplace. A cyclist suddenly swerved to avoid a car door that had been carelessly opened, and fell heavily onto the road. Several people immediately rushed over to help. One woman calmly called for an ambulance while two men carefully helped the cyclist to sit up. Fortunately, he had been wearing a helmet, which had protected him from serious injury. Apparently, the driver of the car had not seen him approaching in the mirror. The ambulance arrived quickly, within about five minutes. Remarkably, the cyclist was able to walk unaided by the time the paramedics got there. The whole incident was over in less than fifteen minutes, but it had clearly shaken everyone who had watched it happen. I have never forgotten how efficiently ordinary people responded that day, without hesitation or panic.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về quá trình học một kỹ năng mới — bạn tiến bộ như thế nào theo thời gian, bạn thường gặp khó khăn ở đâu, và điều gì cuối cùng đã giúp bạn thành công. Sử dụng nhiều loại trạng từ khác nhau để mô tả tiến trình.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Adverbs of frequency, manner, degree, and time in descriptive writing
writing_prompts.example: Learning to drive was one of the most challenging things I have ever done. At first, I struggled constantly with the coordination of clutch, accelerator, and steering, and I rarely managed more than five minutes without making a mistake. My instructor always remained patient, however, which helped me enormously. I practised consistently every weekend, often going over the same manoeuvres repeatedly until they started to feel natural. Gradually, my confidence grew. After about three months, I could already handle most everyday driving situations without panicking. Surprisingly, the parallel parking that I had dreaded most turned out to be the skill I mastered most quickly. By the time my test date arrived, I had improved remarkably compared to where I had started. I passed first time, which I had never genuinely expected. Looking back, I think the key was simply practising hard and not giving up, even when progress felt frustratingly slow.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một sự kiện hoặc tình huống bất ngờ đã xảy ra gần đây — điều gì xảy ra, ai liên quan, và kết quả ra sao (sử dụng trạng từ ở đầu câu, giữa câu, và cuối câu để tạo văn phong phong phú)
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Adverb positions: initial (fortunately, suddenly, recently), mid (completely, already, often), final (quickly, carefully, well, hard)
writing_prompts.example: Last week, something completely unexpected happened at work. I had been sitting quietly at my desk when suddenly the fire alarm went off. Obviously, everyone had to leave the building immediately. Fortunately, nobody panicked — people moved quickly and calmly towards the exits. Apparently, someone had accidentally triggered the alarm while using the kitchen microwave. The building was thoroughly checked and everyone was soon allowed back inside. Unfortunately, we had already lost nearly two hours of work time, and several important deadlines had to be pushed back. The manager spoke briefly but clearly to the team and reassured everyone that the situation had been handled well. Interestingly, the incident brought the team closer together — we had never chatted so openly before. Apparently, it sometimes takes an unexpected event to remind people that they genuinely enjoy working together.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một người nổi tiếng hoặc nhân vật trong sách mà bạn ngưỡng mộ — họ hành động như thế nào, họ làm mọi việc ra sao, và điều gì làm bạn ấn tượng nhất (chú ý dùng đúng vị trí trạng từ cách thức, tần suất và bình luận)
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Manner adverbs (carefully, brilliantly, hard, well), frequency (always, never, rarely), comment (obviously, apparently, remarkably)
writing_prompts.example: The historical figure I most admire is Marie Curie, whose story never fails to inspire me. She worked tirelessly in her laboratory, often spending more than twelve hours a day conducting experiments. She always approached problems methodically and carefully, recording every detail with remarkable precision. Apparently, she rarely took holidays, even during the most demanding periods of her research. Remarkably, she managed to raise two daughters while simultaneously pursuing groundbreaking science — something that was obviously considered almost impossible at the time. She spoke clearly and confidently in academic settings, despite frequently being the only woman in the room. She never sought public attention and apparently found the fame that followed her Nobel Prizes deeply uncomfortable. Obviously, what makes her truly exceptional is not only what she achieved scientifically, but how completely devoted she was to the pursuit of knowledge for its own sake.
